He returned 4 actual pts (8 with captain multiplier), underperforming his prediction, but the model's logic was sound given his fixture and form.
player_explanations.Haaland: Haaland was predicted 4.5 pts — matching the FWD group average of 4.5 pts exactly — but at £14.9m his £/pred ratio of 3.31 made him by far the most expensive asset relative to predicted output in the squad. The ILP selected him because his medium Fulham away fixture (FDR 3/5) gave a reasonable scoring probability and his elite ceiling as a premium FWD means even a moderate prediction carries high upside potential. He returned 5 actual pts, marginally beating his prediction, a solid if unspectacular return that justified his selection over the injury-doubtful bench alternative.

gameweeks.captain_note: The model correctly identified Borges Fernandes as the top captaincy pick with the highest predicted score of 6.5 pts, ahead of Muñoz Mejía (5.9), Rogers (5.1), and Bowen (4.6) — a 0.6 pt gap over the second option that made him the clear ILP choice for the armband. His home fixture against West Ham (FDR 2/5 easy) was the key driver, giving the model the highest clean-sheet-irrelevant attacking probability among all candidates, and at £8.9m his premium price reflected his consistent output. In hindsight, Muñoz Mejía's 14 actual pts (which would have yielded 28 with the captaincy) versus Borges Fernandes's 4 actual pts (8 with captain) shows the model missed the true standout performer, but with only a 0.6 pt predicted gap and Muñoz Mejía's prediction of 5.9 pts, the data-driven case for captaining Borges Fernandes over the Crystal Palace defender was statistically reasonable at selection time.
